description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with an irregular, pitted surface and a light beige to gray coloration. The block has a coarse, porous texture, with visible natural indentations and some minor chipping along the edges. Its form is not perfectly geometric, with one side appearing slightly more angular and another more rounded, suggesting it was quarried or broken rather than precisely cut. The surface shows signs of weathering, with small pits and a generally matte finish. There are faint, possibly man-made markings on one end, but no deep engravings or carvings. The block is solid and heavy, suitable for use as a barrier or structural element in an urban or landscaped environment.
description: A heavy, irregularly shaped stone block with a rough, pitted beige-gray surface and minor edge chipping. It appears weathered and solid, suitable for use as a barrier or structural element.
description_view_0: The broadest face of the stone block, showing its rough, pitted texture and irregular outline with some edge chipping.
description_view_1: A narrow, angular end of the block, highlighting its thickness and the coarse, porous surface.
description_view_2: The opposite broad face, similar in texture to view 0 but with a more pronounced central seam and less edge chipping.
description_view_3: The other narrow end, showing a rounded profile and faint surface markings near the top.
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface is pitted and rough, with minor chipping and signs of natural weathering. No significant moss or water smoothing is visible.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Faint surface markings are visible on one end, but no deep engravings or inscriptions.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Isolated or loosely aligned with other blocks as a barrier or edging element
